question_text: Suppose that $X _ { 1 } , X _ { 2 } , \ldots , X _ { n }$ are independent and identically distributed random variables each having probability density function $f ( \cdot )$ and median θ. We want to test $ H _ { 0 } : \theta = \theta _ { 0 } \quad \mathrm { a g a i n s t } \quad H _ { 1 } : \theta > \theta _ { 0 } . $

options.C: onsider a test that rejects $H _ { 0 } \mathbf { i f } \ S > c$ for some c depending on the size of the test, where $S$ is the cardinality of the set $\{ i : X _ { i } > \theta _ { 0 } , 1 \le i \le n \}$ . Then which one of the following statements is true?
options.A: Under $H _ { 0 } ,$ , the distribution of S depends on $f ( \cdot )$
options.B: Under $H _ { 1 }$ , the distribution of S does not depend on $f ( \cdot )$
options.D: The power function does not depend on θ

solution:
1) Understanding the power function:

The power function of a test is defined as the probability of rejecting $H _ { 0 }$ when the true parameter is $\theta , { \mathrm { i . e . , ~ } } \beta ( \theta ) = P ( \operatorname { R e j e c t i n g } H _ { 0 } \mid \theta )$ . The power function depends on how the test behaves under different values of θ.
2) Analysis:

\- The test uses the statistic $S ,$ the number of $X _ { i }$ greater than $\theta _ { 0 }$ . Under $H _ { 0 }$ , this statistic follows a binomial distribution with parameters n and the probability $P ( X _ { i } > \theta _ { 0 } )$ . - The distribution of S is affected by the value of $\theta ,$ and so is the power of the test. Hence, the power function depends on θ.

Thus, the correct answer is (C).
Quick Tip

\- The power function of a hypothesis test depends on how the test behaves for different values of the parameter θ under consideration.

\- A test’s power increases as the distance between the null hypothesis and alternative hypothesis increases.
